headline: What if chronic pain could be unlearned?
body:
Most people are told that chronic pain means ongoing damage.

But modern neuroscience tells a different story.
caption:
Most people are told that chronic pain means ongoing damage. Modern neuroscience tells a different story.

Pain is produced by the brain, not simply reported by it. When symptoms persist, the nervous system can learn pain as a habit, firing the alarm even after tissue has healed. The same wiring that learned the pain can be taught to settle.

This is the basis of recovery for many people living with chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID. It does not mean the pain is imagined. It means it is real, and it is changeable.

Ready to start? Visit https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #NervousSystem #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Background reading: Moseley GL and Butler DS (2015), Fifteen Years of Explaining Pain: The Past, Present, and Future, The Journal of Pain, 16(9), 807 to 813. A landmark review of how teaching people the biology of pain reduces its intensity and disability. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jpain.2015.05.005
